comments.body:
🤖 Auto-generated draft — probation mode (not sent to customer)
Product: shopify-mcsl | Signal: keyword | Confidence: MEDIUM
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Hello Ibrahim,

Thank you for reaching out. To help you get labels generated for orders 68951 and 68952, please start with these checks:

1. Carrier connection — Go to MCSL app → Carriers and confirm the carrier you are trying to use shows a Connected status. If it shows Disconnected or an error, reconnecting the account usually resolves label failures.

2. Order data — Open each order in the MCSL app and verify the Ship To address is complete (no missing city, postcode, or country), all products have a weight assigned, and the order is in a paid or fulfilled status.

3. Carrier balance (if using Stamps.com or USPS) — Make sure the account has sufficient postage balance before retrying.

4. Retry the label — After confirming the above, click Generate Label again on each order.

If the issue persists, please share the exact error message or status shown in the MCSL app when you attempt to generate the label for these orders, along with the carrier you are using. That will allow us to identify the specific cause and resolve it quickly.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

⚡ This response was generated automatically by PluginHive AI Support. A team member will follow up if you need further assistance.

comments.body:
🤖 Auto-generated draft — customer reply (probation mode)
Product: shopify-mcsl | Signal: form_field | Confidence: MEDIUM | Context: 4 message(s)
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Hello Ibrahim,

Thank you for confirming the error and for granting us permission to generate the labels.

As our team noted, the issue is that your app is currently set to use pounds (LBS), but UPS requires metric units (KGS) for shipments to Germany. When the app sends the label request using pounds, UPS rejects it with the "Invalid Unit of Measurement for the given country" error. This is also the likely cause of some of your Canada orders failing — UPS enforces metric unit requirements for certain services and destinations.

To fix this for future orders, please follow these steps:

1. Open the MCSL app and go to App Settings > General.
2. Change the weight unit from LBS to KG.
3. Save the settings.

Once this is updated, please also verify that your product weights in the app reflect values in kilograms, as this change will affect how weights are sent to UPS for all orders going forward.

Our team will proceed with generating the labels for orders 68951 and 68952 from our end and will update you once done. In the meantime, updating the weight unit setting will prevent this error from recurring on future international orders.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

⚡ This response was generated automatically by PluginHive AI Support. A team member will follow up if you need further assistance.

Hello Ibrahim Hatoum,

Thank you for your permission.

We will be escalating your case so that it can be checked further by our senior team.

Before that could you please let us know if these 2 orders can wait for some time (say about 24 hours) so that our team can check and update you accordingly?
If the orders have to be shipped urgently, you can process them outside of the app for now and do not fulfill the order in Shopify.

Awaiting your confirmation.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1781537819928199

US Customer using MCSL-S with UPS for labels.
Customer shared Orders 68951 and 68952 which are in Initial Status in the app.
Upon checking, we observed that the UPS rates are failing with error "**Invalid Unit of Measurement for the given country**"
The unit of measurement being passed in the request is LBS. All the Order details available in the app are in Kg and cms.
I have tried reprocessing the orders in the app and verified that the store Units are set to Kilogram and the packaging is also set as Kilograms, but the rates are still failing with the same error.
Orders before these and after these are processing without any issues.
Could you please check and let us know how this can be resolved in the app?

URL - localeuropean.myshopify.com
Account ID - bfbbb21c-18eb-45b4-aa28-1f0e32430b71

Hello Ibrahim Hatoum,

Thank you for your patience.

We have checked your case related to Orders 68951 and 68952 and the problem has been identified. However, to resolve this case, some enhancement will be needed in the app from our end.

We will be enhancing the app and updating you soon.

Regarding the orders, if they are to be shipped soon, I request you to generate the labels for them outside the app on ups.com

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
